mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single calendar month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer credit outstanding and industrial production indicate synchronized demand-supply contraction, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline of both series does not predict negative 60d returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 250, federal funds rate increases act as a tightening shock that disproportionately penalizes duration-sensitive equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases fail to produce negative forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth does not correlate with positive 5d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1000 thousand units signals a sharp residential demand contraction that historically leads to negative equity returns over 60 trading days due to wealth effect drag and construction sector earnings risk.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1000 does not precede negative 60d SPX returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates supply-side productivity gains that expand corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint rise in INDPRO and stable UNRATE fails to predict positive 20d returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods where real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0 percent annualized, declines in consumer credit amplify recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration combined with RSAFS decline does not predict negative 5d returns in >55% of observations.
